CREATE TABLE test_table (number Int32, index UInt8);
SELECT number, index, bitTest(number, index) AS bit_value FROM test_table WHERE number = 43;
SELECT bitTest(256, 8) AS bit_value UNION SELECT bitTest(256, 9);
WITH (SELECT number FROM test_table WHERE number < 50) AS subquery SELECT bitTest(number, 0) FROM subquery;
SELECT number, index FROM test_table WHERE bitTest(number, index) = 1;
SELECT bitTest(num, 3) AS bit_value FROM temp_table WHERE num >= 7 AND num <= 10;
SELECT DISTINCT bitTest(43, index) FROM (SELECT index FROM test_table) AS indices;
SELECT bitTest(0, 0) AS zero_bit, bitTest(0, 1) AS higher_bit;
SELECT bitTest(number, index) FROM test_table WHERE index > 30;
CREATE TABLE join_table (id UInt32, number Int32);
SELECT jt.id, jt.number, bt.bit_value FROM join_table jt JOIN (SELECT number, index, bitTest(number, index) AS bit_value FROM test_table) bt ON jt.number = bt.number;
SELECT bitTest((SELECT number FROM test_table WHERE index = 1 LIMIT 1), 1) AS subquery_bit_value;
SELECT bitTest(number, index) FROM (SELECT number, index FROM test_table) GROUP BY number, index HAVING bitTest(number, index) = 1;
